task_title: Reformat slide title and body text in LibreOffice Impress
instruction: Underline the slide title and change its font size to 50 pt.
task_goal: The title text is fully selected, underlined, and updated to a 50-point size.

steps.step_goal: Activate title text editing Select the entire title
action_preconditions: A slide with the title "The History of Art" and an italic bullet paragraph is open in Normal view, and the title box is visible but not active for text editing.
The title text box is already in text-edit mode and the word "of" is selected.

nl_position: the word "of" in the centered title near the top of the slide

steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, labeled as Slide 1 of 1, is titled "The History of Art" and contains a bullet point defining art as a diverse range of human activities creating visual, auditory, or performing artifacts for aesthetic or emotional appreciation. The slide is in “Normal” view, with the Properties panel open on the right showing settings like Format (On-screen w:4:3), Orientation (Landscape), Background (None), and Master Slide (Blank Slide). Layout options are visible below the Properties panel. The left sidebar displays slide thumbnails, with the current slide selected. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The system clock at the top indicates it is Mar 25, 17:53. A red crosshair cursor is positioned over the word “of” in the title. The bottom status bar shows zoom level (100%) and slide dimensions. The Ubuntu desktop environment is visible in the background with various application icons on the left dock.
action_after_effects: The full title "The History of Art" becomes highlighted for formatting.
steps.nl_explanation: Double-click the word "of" in the title to enter text-edit mode and select that word. Click the selected title word again to expand the selection from one word to the full title.

action_preconditions: The complete title text is selected inside the title box.
steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress presentation editor open with a slide titled "The History of Art". The title text is selected, showing font settings (Times New Roman, 42 pt) in the Properties panel on the right. Below the title is a bullet point defining art as a diverse range of human activities creating visual, auditory, or performing artifacts for beauty or emotional power. The slide is slide 1 of 1. The left sidebar shows slide thumbnails, and the top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The status bar at bottom indicates text editing mode and zoom level.
action_after_effects: An underline is applied to the selected title while it remains selected.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Ctrl+U to underline the selected title.

steps.step_goal: Focus the title font size field
action_preconditions: The underlined title is still selected, and the Character panel on the right shows the size control currently set to 42 pt.

nl_position: the font size input showing "42 pt" near the top of the right-side Character panel

steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, labeled as Slide 1 of 1, has a title "The History of Art" in bold, underlined text with a light pink background. Below the title is a bullet point defining art as a diverse range of human activities creating visual, auditory, or performing artifacts for beauty or emotional power. The Properties panel on the right is open to the Character section, showing font settings (Times New Roman) and formatting options like bold, italic, underline, and font size. The toolbar at the top includes standard menu items such as File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The left sidebar displays slide thumbnails, with the current slide selected. The status bar at the bottom indicates the slide number, text editing mode, dimensions, and language setting (English USA). The system clock in the top bar reads Mar 25 17:53.
action_after_effects: The font size box receives focus and is ready for keyboard input.
steps.nl_explanation: Click the font size field in the Character panel to edit the selected title's size.

steps.step_goal: Select the current font size value
action_preconditions: The font size input for the selected title is focused and shows 42 pt.
steps.action_before_state:
The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, labeled as Slide 1 of 1, has a title "The History of Art" in bold, underlined text. Below the title is a bullet point defining art as a diverse range of human activities involving visual, auditory, or performing artifacts created to express imaginative or conceptual ideas and appreciated for beauty or emotional power.

The interface includes the standard menu bar (File, Edit, View, etc.) and toolbar with icons for formatting and editing. On the right side, the Properties panel is open, showing tabs for Character, Lists, and Paragraph settings. The Character tab is active, displaying font options (currently set to Times New Roman) and font size (set to 40). The Paragraph section shows spacing and indent values all set to 0.00 cm. The left sidebar displays thumbnails of slides, with the current slide visible. The system clock at the top indicates the time as Mar 25 17:53. The bottom status bar shows slide navigation details and zoom level (100%). The application window is maximized on a desktop environment with a visible dock on the left side containing application icons.
action_after_effects: The existing font size value in the input is selected for replacement.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Ctrl+A to select the current value in the active font size field.

steps.step_goal: Enter a new title font size
action_preconditions: The current font size value in the active size field is selected and ready to be overwritten.
steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open with a presentation titled “69_4.pptx”. The active slide shows a title “The History of Art” in bold, underlined, Times New Roman font, highlighted with a light orange background. Below it is a bullet point defining “Art” as a diverse range of human activities involving visual, auditory, or performing artifacts, emphasizing beauty or emotional power. The left sidebar displays the slide thumbnail for Slide 1. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The Properties panel on the right is open to the Character section, showing font settings (Times New Roman), bold/italic/underline, font color, and other formatting options. The status bar at the bottom indicates “Slide 1 of 1”, “TextEdit: Paragraph 1, Row 1, Column 19”, and zoom level at 100%. The system time shown is Mar 25 17:53.
action_after_effects: The size field changes from the old value to 50, but the title on the slide has not yet updated.
steps.nl_explanation: Type 50 to replace the current title font size.

steps.step_goal: Apply the new title font size
action_preconditions: The size field contains the new value 50 for the selected title.
steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, Slide 1 of 1, is in edit mode and displays a title, “The History of Art”, formatted in bold with an underline and set against a light pink background. Below the title is a bullet point defining art as a diverse range of human activities creating visual, auditory, or performing artifacts for aesthetic or emotional appreciation. The text is in Times New Roman font, size 50, as shown in the Properties panel on the right. The Properties panel also displays tabs for Character, Lists, and Paragraph formatting, with spacing and indent values currently at 0.00 cm. The main menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The left sidebar shows thumbnails of slides, with the current slide selected. The system clock indicates it is 17:53 on March 25. The interface is running on a Linux desktop environment, as indicated by the dock icons on the left side of the screen.
action_after_effects: The title updates to a larger 50 pt underlined heading on the slide.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Enter to apply the 50 pt size to the title.
